facts.claim: Intensicare is a clinical decision support platform for Brazilian ICUs (UTI), operating on the AMH Data Platform (lakehouse with Apache Iceberg, MPI, and FHIR R4), ingesting vital signs, lab results, and EMR data to generate intelligent alerts and evidence-based recommendations.
Phase 1 (MVP) implemented early-warning scores: MEWS (Modified Early Warning Score, general clinical deterioration) — status Implementado.
Phase 1 implemented NEWS2 (National Early Warning Score 2, NHS UK), including the hypercapnic (Scale 2) variant — status Implementado.
Phase 1 implemented SOFA (Sequential Organ Failure Assessment, daily organ dysfunction) — status Implementado.
Phase 1 implemented qSOFA (Quick SOFA, bedside sepsis triage) — status Implementado.
Phase 1 ingestion pillar: HL7 v2 via MLLP listener in Python -> JSON -> FastAPI API.
Phase 1 processing pillar: deterministic Python functions; explicitly no ML runtime in the MVP.
Phase 1 storage pillar: PostgreSQL + TimescaleDB (hypertables for time series).
Phase 1 queue/cache pillar: Redis (state cache, rate limiting, pub/sub for alerts).
Phase 1 interface pillar: REST API (FastAPI) + WebSocket for real-time push.
Sepsis justification: sepsis is the #1 cause of death in Brazilian ICUs (incidence ~30%, mortality ~55% per ILAS); early detection (<1h from identification) reduces mortality by 25-30%.
Alert 'SIRS + suspeita de infecção': fires on >=2 SIRS criteria + positive/presumed culture. Evidence: Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021.
Alert 'qSOFA >=2 com tendência de lactato': fires on qSOFA >=2 AND (lactate > 2 mmol/L OR delta lactate > 0.5 mmol/L/h). Evidence: Seymour et al., JAMA 2016.
Alert 'Choque séptico iminente': fires on lactate >= 4 mmol/L + MAP < 65 mmHg despite fluids. Evidence: SSC 2021 1-hour Bundle.
Alert 'Procalcitonina em elevação': fires on PCT > 0.5 ng/mL + delta > 0.25 ng/mL in 24h. Evidence: Schuetz et al., Lancet ID 2018.
Alert 'Desmame antimicrobiano': fires on PCT < 0.25 ng/mL OR drop > 80% from peak + 48h of stability. Evidence: PROGRESS Trial, JAMA 2023.
Sepsis domain required data: vital signs (HR, RR, temperature, BP), WBC/leukogram, serum lactate, procalcitonin, cultures, MAP.
Sepsis domain integration: FHIR Observation (vitals + labs), FHIR DiagnosticReport (cultures), FHIR MedicationRequest (antimicrobials).
AKI justification: AKI occurs in 30-60% of ICU patients and is associated with a 6.5x increase in mortality; early KDIGO-based diagnosis enables intervention at reversible stages.
Alert 'KDIGO Estágio 1': fires on Cr increase >= 0.3 mg/dL in 48h OR Cr >= 1.5x baseline OR urine output < 0.5 mL/kg/h for 6h. Evidence: KDIGO 2012 Guidelines.
Alert 'KDIGO Estágio 2': fires on Cr >= 2.0x baseline OR urine output < 0.5 mL/kg/h for 12h. Evidence: KDIGO 2012.
Alert 'KDIGO Estágio 3': fires on Cr >= 3.0x baseline OR Cr >= 4.0 mg/dL with acute increase >= 0.5 mg/dL OR urine output < 0.3 mL/kg/h for 24h OR anuria for 12h. Evidence: KDIGO 2012.
Alert 'Progressão AKI': fires on a KDIGO stage change within a 24h window. Evidence: KDIGO 2012.
Alert 'Nefrotoxicidade por drogas': fires on initiation of vancomycin + aminoglycoside + contrast + rising creatinine. Evidence: AIN Consensus 2020.
AKI domain required data: serial serum creatinine, hourly urine output, patient weight, nephrotoxic medications.
AKI domain integration: FHIR Observation (creatinine, diuresis), FHIR MedicationRequest, fluid balance from the multiparameter monitor.
Respiratory failure justification: SpO2/FiO2 ratio monitoring detects respiratory deterioration equivalent to PaO2/FiO2 without repeated invasive arterial blood gas collection.
Alert 'SDRA Leve (Berlin)': fires on SpO2/FiO2 <= 315 (equiv. PaO2/FiO2 200-300) OR PaO2/FiO2 200-300 + bilateral infiltrates. Evidence: Berlin Definition, JAMA 2012.
Alert 'SDRA Moderada': fires on SpO2/FiO2 <= 235 (equiv. PaO2/FiO2 100-200) OR PaO2/FiO2 100-200. Evidence: Berlin Definition.
Alert 'SDRA Grave': fires on SpO2/FiO2 <= 148 (equiv. PaO2/FiO2 < 100) OR PaO2/FiO2 < 100. Evidence: Berlin Definition.
Alert 'Deterioração ventilatória': fires on drop > 20% in SpO2/FiO2 within 6h OR increase > 30% in FiO2 to maintain target SpO2. Evidence: Rice et al., Chest 2017.
Alert 'Assincronia ventilatória': fires on spontaneous RR > set RR + plateau pressure > 30 cmH2O (if available). Evidence: Thille et al., ICM 2016.
Alert 'Prontidão para desmame': fires on SpO2/FiO2 > 315 + PEEP <= 8 + RR/Vt < 105 (RSBI) stable for 2h. Evidence: Boles et al., ERJ 2017.
Respiratory domain required data: continuous SpO2, ventilator FiO2, PaO2 and PaCO2 (blood gas), respiratory rate, tidal volume (Vt), PEEP, plateau pressure.
Respiratory domain integration: FHIR Observation (SpO2, blood gas), mechanical ventilator data via HL7 ORU (manufacturer-specific OBX segments).
Hemodynamic instability justification: unrecognized circulatory shock has mortality > 80%; combined indices (shock index, lactate clearance) outperform isolated blood-pressure monitoring.
Alert 'Shock Index elevado': fires on HR/SBP > 0.9 sustained for > 15 min. Evidence: Rady et al., Ann Emerg Med 1994.
Alert 'Shock Index modificado': fires on HR/MAP > 1.3 sustained for > 15 min. Evidence: Liu et al., Crit Care 2012.
Alert 'Clearance de lactato inadequado': fires on clearance < 10% in 2h OR lactate persistently > 2 mmol/L after 6h of resuscitation. Evidence: Jones et al., JAMA 2010.
Alert 'Vasopressor escalating': fires on increase > 50% in norepinephrine dose within 2h OR addition of a 2nd vasopressor. Evidence: SCCM 2024 Guidelines.
Alert 'Hipotensão permissiva não controlada': fires on MAP < 65 mmHg for > 30 min despite maximal vasopressor (e.g., norepinephrine > 1 μg/kg/min). Evidence: SEPSISPAM Trial, NEJM 2014.
Alert 'Fluid responsiveness negativo': fires on pulse pressure variation (PPV) < 10% + fluid challenge without response (stroke volume increase < 10%). Evidence: Marik et al., Crit Care Med 2013.
Hemodynamic domain required data: HR, systolic/diastolic/mean BP, serial lactate, vasopressor doses (drug + infusion rate), pulse pressure variation (PPV), stroke volume (if monitor available).
Hemodynamic domain integration: FHIR Observation (BP, HR, lactate), FHIR MedicationAdministration (vasopressors with infusion rate), advanced hemodynamic monitor data.

Delirium justification: delirium occurs in 50-80% of mechanically ventilated patients, is associated with a 3x increase in mortality, prolonged mechanical ventilation, and long-term cognitive decline; structured screening reduces delirium duration by 1-2 days.
Alert 'RASS alvo fora da faixa': fires on RASS > +1 (agitation) or RASS < -3 (deep sedation) in a patient without indication for deep sedation. Evidence: PADIS Guidelines, ICM 2018.
Alert 'CAM-ICU positivo': fires on CAM-ICU positive (acute change + inattention + RASS != 0 + disorganized thinking). Evidence: Ely et al., NEJM 2001.
Alert 'Risco de delirium iatrogênico': fires on continuous-infusion benzodiazepine + age > 65 years + RASS <= -2. Evidence: PADIS 2018, SCCM.
Alert 'Delirium hipoativo não reconhecido': fires on RASS between -1 and -3 + CAM-ICU positive + no sedation in the last 4h. Evidence: van den Boogaard et al., BMJ 2014.
Alert 'Mobilização inadequada': fires on absence of a mobilization record (bedside sitting, dangling, ambulation) for > 24h in an eligible patient. Evidence: TEAM Study, NEJM 2022.
Delirium domain required data: RASS (Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale), CAM-ICU (4 features), sedative medications (drug, dose, route), mobilization record, age.
Delirium domain integration: FHIR Observation (RASS, CAM-ICU), FHIR MedicationAdministration (sedatives), nursing records (mobilization, restraint).

Electrolyte emergencies justification: severe K+/Na+/Ca2+ disturbances are potentially fatal within minutes to hours; automated detection with critical thresholds reduces time-to-correction and prevents arrhythmias, seizures, and cardiac arrest.
Alert 'Hipercalemia grave': Critical trigger K+ > 6.5 mmol/L; Urgent trigger K+ > 6.0 mmol/L. Evidence: UKKA 2023 Guidelines.
Alert 'Hipocalemia grave': Critical trigger K+ < 2.5 mmol/L; Urgent trigger K+ < 3.0 mmol/L. Evidence: UKKA 2023.
Alert 'Hipernatremia grave': Critical trigger Na+ > 160 mmol/L; Urgent trigger Na+ > 155 mmol/L. Evidence: ESICM 2024 Consensus.
Alert 'Hiponatremia grave': Critical trigger Na+ < 120 mmol/L; Urgent trigger Na+ < 125 mmol/L. Evidence: ESICM 2024.
Alert 'Delta Na+ perigoso': Critical trigger Na+ correction > 10 mmol/L in 24h (risk of central pontine myelinolysis); Urgent trigger correction > 8 mmol/L in 24h. Evidence: Sterns et al., JASN 2015.
Alert 'Hipercalcemia grave': Critical trigger ionized Ca2+ > 1.60 mmol/L (or corrected total Ca > 14 mg/dL); Urgent trigger Ca2+ > 1.45 mmol/L. Evidence: Mousseaux et al., NDT 2022.
Alert 'Hipocalcemia grave': Critical trigger ionized Ca2+ < 0.80 mmol/L (or corrected total Ca < 7.0 mg/dL); Urgent trigger Ca2+ < 0.90 mmol/L. Evidence: Cooper et al., ICM 2022.
Alert 'Hipomagnesemia': Critical trigger Mg2+ < 0.5 mmol/L (1.2 mg/dL); Urgent trigger Mg2+ < 0.7 mmol/L (1.7 mg/dL). Evidence: Hansen et al., ICM 2018.
Electrolyte domain required data: K+, Na+, Ca2+ (total and ionized), Mg2+, albumin (for Ca correction), creatinine/clearance (for Na correction).
Electrolyte domain integration: FHIR Observation (electrolyte panel), electrolyte replacement data via FHIR MedicationAdministration.

Drug interactions justification: ICU patients receive on average 12-20 simultaneous medications; serious drug interactions (contraindications, QTc prolongation, serotonin syndrome) account for 5-10% of ICU adverse events.
Alert 'QTc prolongamento — risco de Torsades': fires on QTc > 500 ms (or delta > 60 ms) + >=2 drugs with known Torsades risk (CredibleMeds list). Evidence: Tisdale et al., Circ 2013.

Alert 'Síndrome serotoninérgica': fires on >=2 serotonergic agents (SSRI + linezolid + fentanyl + ondansetron + metoclopramide) + clonus/hyperthermia/hyperreflexia. Evidence: Boyer & Shannon, NEJM 2005.
Alert 'Nefrotoxicidade aditiva': fires on vancomycin + aminoglycoside + NSAID + iodinated contrast within 72h. Evidence: KDIGO Drug-Induced AKI 2023.
Alert 'Depressão respiratória sinérgica': fires on >=2 CNS depressants (opioid + benzodiazepine + gabapentinoid) + RR < 10 or SpO2 < 90%. Evidence: Overdyk et al., Anesth Analg 2016.
Alert 'Duplicidade terapêutica': fires on 2+ medications of the same class (e.g., 2 PPIs, 2 antiemetics, 2 anticoagulants). Evidence: ISMP Guidelines 2023.
Alert 'Síndrome de abstinência': fires on abrupt discontinuation of BZD/opioid after > 7 days continuous use + autonomic symptoms. Evidence: SCCM PADIS 2018.
Alert 'Antimicrobiano sem ajuste renal': fires on renally-cleared antimicrobial + CrCl < 30 mL/min without dose adjustment. Evidence: Rybak et al., AJHP 2020 (vancomycin).

Drug-interaction domain required data: complete active medication list (drug, dose, route, frequency, start date), ECG (QTc), RR, SpO2, creatinine + estimated clearance, documented symptoms (clonus, rigidity, hyperthermia).
Drug-interaction domain integration: FHIR MedicationRequest + MedicationAdministration, FHIR Observation (ECG, creatinine, SpO2), FHIR Condition (diagnosis records).
AMH Data Platform box contains 4 components — MPI (Master Patient Index), FHIR R4 (Store), Iceberg (Lakehouse Tables), FHIR API (Gateway) — all feeding Intensicare via the FHIR REST API.

Intensicare's Alert Engine (Fase 2) contains 7 detector boxes: Sepsis Detector, AKI Detector, Respiratory Failure Detector, Hemodynamic Detector, Delirium Detector, Electrolyte Critical Detector, Drug-Drug Interaction Detector.

Correlation Engine (multi-domain) box inside the Alert Engine defines exactly 3 cross-domain correlations: (1) Sepsis + AKI — 'sepse é #1 causa de AKI'; (2) Respiratory + Hemodynamic — 'SDRA + choque'; (3) Drug + Electrolyte — 'QTc + K+/Mg2+'.
Intensicare's persistence layer under the Alert Engine: TimescaleDB (time series + scores + alerts) and Redis (state cache, rate limiting, pub/sub).
Data sources — Sepse domain: AMH source FHIR Observation (vitals, labs, PCT, cultures); local TimescaleDB source vitals streaming via HL7 ORU; frequency every 5 min / on lab result.
Data sources — AKI domain: AMH source FHIR Observation (Cr, diurese); local TimescaleDB source hourly fluid balance; frequency every 1-6h.
Data sources — Respiratória domain: AMH source FHIR Observation (blood gas); local TimescaleDB source ventilator data via HL7; frequency every 5 min.
Data sources — Hemodinâmica domain: AMH source FHIR Observation (lactate); local TimescaleDB source invasive monitor (continuous BP); frequency continuous.
Data sources — Delirium domain: AMH source FHIR Observation (RASS, CAM-ICU); local TimescaleDB source nursing record; frequency every 4-12h.
Data sources — Eletrólitos domain: AMH source FHIR Observation (electrolyte panel); local TimescaleDB source none ('—'); frequency on lab result.
Data sources — Drogas domain: AMH source FHIR MedicationRequest / MedicationAdministration; local TimescaleDB source none ('—'); frequency on order/administration.
Priority matrix P1 = Sepse / Infecção: Impacto Clínico Crítico; Disponibilidade de Dados Alta (vitals + labs via FHIR); Complexidade Média.
Priority matrix P2 = AKI (KDIGO): Impacto Clínico Crítico; Disponibilidade de Dados Alta (creatinina + diurese); Complexidade Baixa.
Priority matrix P3 = Emergências Eletrolíticas: Impacto Clínico Alto; Disponibilidade de Dados Alta (painel lab via FHIR); Complexidade Baixa.
Priority matrix P4 = Instabilidade Hemodinâmica: Impacto Clínico Crítico; Disponibilidade de Dados Média (lactato, PA invasiva); Complexidade Média.
Priority matrix P5 = Insuficiência Respiratória: Impacto Clínico Alto; Disponibilidade de Dados Média (depende de dados do ventilador); Complexidade Média.
Priority matrix P6 = Interações Medicamentosas: Impacto Clínico Moderado; Disponibilidade de Dados Baixa (requer integração completa EMR); Complexidade Alta.
Priority matrix P7 = Delirium: Impacto Clínico Moderado; Disponibilidade de Dados Baixa (depende de registro clínico estruturado); Complexidade Média.
Fase 2a, Meses 1-3: Sepse (SIRS, qSOFA, lactato) + AKI (KDIGO) + Eletrólitos críticos.
Fase 2b, Meses 4-6: Hemodinâmica (shock index, clearance lactato) + Respiratória (SpO2/FiO2).
Fase 2c, Meses 7-9: Interações medicamentosas + Delirium (CAM-ICU triggers).
Fase 2d, Meses 10-12: Correlation Engine multi-domínio + ML preditivo (sepse, AKI).

Before-After study design: control period 3 months (conventional MEWS/NEWS monitoring, no Phase 2 alerts, retrospective data) + intervention period 3 months (Phase 2 alerts active with team notification) + 2-week washout for training/calibration.

Before-After primary outcomes and goals: time-to-antibiotic in sepsis (goal: reduce by 60 min); time-to-recognition of AKI KDIGO >=1 (goal: reduce by 6h); incidence of cardiac arrest from electrolyte disturbance (goal: reduce by 50%).
Before-After secondary outcomes: alert fired-vs-confirmed rate (PPV), clinical response time (time-to-action), 28-day ICU mortality, ventilator-free days, ICU-free days.
Before-After inclusion criteria: ICU patients age >= 18 years, expected stay > 24h, at least 1 set of vital signs + 1 lab result in the first 24h.
Before-After sample size: 2 ICUs (30 beds each) x 3 months = ~540 admissions per period; power 80%, alpha = 0.05, to detect a 20% reduction in time-to-antibiotic.

Before-After statistical analysis: paired t-test or Mann-Whitney for continuous variables; chi-square or Fisher for categorical variables; interrupted time series (ITS) analysis for temporal trends; propensity-score adjustment for confounders (age, SAPS 3, comorbidities).

Stepped-wedge design: stepped-wedge cluster randomized controlled trial (SW-CRT); 8 ICU clusters (4 hospitals, 2 ICUs per hospital); total period 18 months; 4 transition steps (3 clusters migrate to intervention per period).
Stepped-wedge randomization: clusters are randomized for transition timing (step); all clusters start in control and all end in intervention.

Stepped-wedge hierarchical primary outcomes: (1) time to adequate antibiotic in sepsis (hours); (2) incidence of AKI KDIGO >=2 during ICU stay; (3) serious adverse events from unrecognized electrolyte disturbance.

Stepped-wedge secondary outcomes: 28-day ICU mortality; mechanical ventilation duration; ICU length of stay; alert burden (alerts/patient/day); alarm-fatigue rate (ignored alerts / total alerts); hospital costs (total ICU stay cost).

Stepped-wedge sample size: 8 clusters x ~45 eligible admissions/month x 18 months = ~6,480 patients; power > 90%, alpha = 0.05 (adjusted for intracluster correlation ICC = 0.02); detects a 25% reduction in the composite primary outcome.

Stepped-wedge statistical analysis: generalized linear mixed model (GLMM) with random effects for cluster; Gamma distribution (log-link) for time-to-event, binomial for incidence; covariates age, sex, SAPS 3, comorbidities, admission type; intention-to-treat (ITT) primary analysis; per-protocol sensitivity analysis adjusted for alert adherence.

Stepped-wedge ethical approval: CEP (institutional Research Ethics Committee) + CONEP (National Research Ethics Commission); registration in ReBEC and ClinicalTrials.gov; individual-consent waiver (institutional intervention) with opt-out; independent DSMB (Data Safety Monitoring Board) with quarterly meetings.
Clinical metric 'Sensibilidade para sepse (detecção em < 1h)': baseline (Fase 1) 45% (NEWS/qSOFA isolados); goal (Fase 2) >= 80%.
Clinical metric 'PPV dos alertas (alertas acionáveis / total)': baseline 35%; goal >= 60%.
Clinical metric 'Tempo médio até ação clínica pós-alerta': baseline 42 min; goal <= 15 min.
Clinical metric 'Taxa de alarm fatigue (alertas ignorados)': baseline 25%; goal <= 10%.
Clinical metric 'Redução de mortalidade em UTI': baseline is the Fase 1 baseline; goal -10% (relative reduction).
Technical metric 'Latência ingestão -> alerta (p95)': goal < 30 seconds.
Technical metric 'Disponibilidade da plataforma': goal 99.9%.
Technical metric 'Throughput de processamento': goal > 500 alerts/min.
Technical metric 'Retenção de dados (TimescaleDB)': goal 7 years (LGPD/CFM compliance).
Technical metric 'Versionamento de algoritmos de alerta': goal 100% auditable.

ANVISA classification: intelligent alerts that do not perform automatic diagnosis nor replace clinical judgment are classified as Software como Dispositivo Médico (SaMD) Classe II under RDC 657/2022, requiring: ANVISA cadastro (not full registro), Boas Práticas de Fabricação (BPF) per RDC 16/2013, and labeling/instructions for use in Portuguese.

LGPD framing: health data is sensitive data (Art. 5º II); specific purpose is clinical decision support (Art. 6º I); legal basis is protection of life/physical safety (Art. 7º VII) plus execution of public health policy (Art. 7º III) if a public hospital; a Relatório de Impacto à Proteção de Dados (RIPD) is mandatory.

CFM framing: Resolução CFM 2.299/2021 governs electronic health record interoperability; alerts must be recorded in the patient's chart at NGS (Nível de Garantia de Segurança) Level 2; the physician remains responsible for the final clinical decision.

Reference list (10 items, verbatim): 1) Seymour CW et al., JAMA 2016;315(8):762-774 (Assessment of Clinical Criteria for Sepsis). 2) Evans L et al., Intensive Care Med 2021;47:1181-1247 (Surviving Sepsis Campaign 2021). 3) KDIGO, Kidney Int Suppl 2012;2(1):1-138 (AKI Guideline). 4) ARDS Definition Task Force, JAMA 2012;307(23):2526-2533 (Berlin Definition). 5) Rady MY et al., Ann Emerg Med 1994;24(4):685-693 (shock index vs vital signs). 6) Ely EW et al., NEJM 2001;345(14):1013-1020 (CAM-ICU validity/reliability). 7) Tisdale JE et al., Circ Cardiovasc Qual Outcomes 2013;6(4):479-487 (QTc risk score). 8) Devlin JW et al., Crit Care Med 2018;46(9):e825-e873 (PADIS Guidelines). 9) Schuetz P et al., Cochrane Database Syst Rev 2017;10:CD007498 (procalcitonin to guide antibiotics). 10) Jones AE et al., JAMA 2010;303(8):739-746 (lactate clearance vs ScvO2).

constraints.text: Intelligent alerts MUST NOT perform automatic diagnosis nor replace clinical judgment; doing so would move the system out of the SaMD Classe II classification described. Verbatim PT: "Alertas inteligentes que não realizam diagnóstico automático nem substituem julgamento clínico".
The alert system MUST be classified/registered as Software como Dispositivo Médico (SaMD) Classe II per RDC 657/2022, requiring ANVISA cadastro (not full registro).
The platform MUST comply with Boas Práticas de Fabricação (BPF) per RDC 16/2013.
Labeling and instructions for use MUST be provided in Portuguese (Rotulagem e instruções de uso em português).
Health data processed MUST be treated as sensitive personal data under LGPD Art. 5º II, with processing limited to the specific purpose of clinical decision support (Art. 6º I).
A Relatório de Impacto à Proteção de Dados (RIPD) MUST be produced (obrigatório) under LGPD.
Every alert MUST be registered in the patient's electronic record (prontuário) at NGS Level 2, per Resolução CFM 2.299/2021.
The physician remains responsible for the final clinical decision; the system's alerts are advisory only. Verbatim PT: "O médico continua sendo responsável pela decisão clínica final."
Platform availability target is 99.9% (Disponibilidade da plataforma).
Data retention in TimescaleDB MUST be 7 years, for LGPD/CFM compliance.
Alert algorithm versioning MUST be 100% auditable.
In the stepped-wedge trial design, all clusters MUST start in control and all MUST end in intervention — no cluster may be denied the intervention (ethical requirement, no privação do benefício).
The stepped-wedge trial MUST have an independent DSMB (Data Safety Monitoring Board) meeting quarterly.
The stepped-wedge trial MUST obtain CEP + CONEP ethical approval and MUST be registered in ReBEC and ClinicalTrials.gov before execution.

open_questions: vision.md does not name which patient/data fields carry the 'delta lactato', 'delta PCT', or 'delta Na+' baseline reference point (e.g., prior value timestamp source) — downstream designers must define the exact lookback mechanics.

vision.md §3.3 does not specify a numeric threshold for P plateau in the SDRA/ventilatory tables beyond the single '> 30 cmH2O' asynchrony cutoff — no plateau-pressure limit is given for the ARDS severity alerts themselves.
vision.md gives no explicit versioning/authorship history beyond the header (Versão 0.2.0-draft, 26 de Junho de 2026, status 'Em expansão') — unclear who owns sign-off on Fase 2 scope.
